[How to upgrade to pro+ plan? - Support / Help - Cursor - Community Forum](https://forum.cursor.com/t/how-to-upgrade-to-pro-plan/109001)<br/><br/># Cursor IDE Subscription Upgrade: $20 to $60 Proration Billing<br/><br/>## Upgrading from Pro to Pro+<br/><br/>To upgrade from Pro ($20/month) to Pro+ ($60/month), use this direct link: `https://www.cursor.com/api/auth/checkoutDeepControl?tier=pro_plus`[1] Pro+ is not displayed on the main pricing page but offers 3x the rate limits for agents.[5]<br/><br/>## Prorated Billing for Upgrades<br/><br/>**How proration works:**[1]<br/>- Your new Pro+ monthly billing starts immediately<br/>- You receive a prorated refund for any unused time on your current plan<br/>- Your previous subscription cancels automatically<br/>- Refunds typically process in 3-5 business days<br/><br/>**Example:** If you paid $120 annually and upgrade after 3 months, you'd be charged the Pro+ rate immediately while receiving a refund for the 9 unused months ($90).[1]<br/><br/>## Important Note<br/><br/>There may be proration issues with the direct checkout link. One Cursor engineer noted the link "may not correctly pro-rate at this time" and recommended canceling your current subscription and waiting for your billing period to end before upgrading to ensure proper billing.[5]<br/><br/>## General Billing Rules<br/><br/>For Business accounts with multiple seats, Cursor uses prorated billing when team size changes, charging only for the actual time each seat is used.[2] You can switch between monthly and annual billing, though switching from yearly to monthly requires contacting support.[2][3] |

[Cursor · Pricing](https://www.cursor.com/en/pricing)<br/><br/># Cursor Pro to Business Plan Upgrade<br/><br/>**Available Plans:**<br/>Cursor offers individual plans (Pro at $20/mo) and business plans. The business option is the Teams plan at $40/user/month, which includes shared chats, centralized billing, usage analytics, SSO, and role-based access control.[4][5]<br/><br/>**Upgrade Process:**<br/>To upgrade, go to cursor.com/dashboard and click the upgrade button for the Teams plan you want.[4] However, users have reported technical issues during the upgrade process, with some encountering checkout errors when attempting to upgrade from Pro to Teams.[1]<br/><br/>**Billing Considerations:**<br/>- When switching plans, you manage your subscription through the dashboard's Billing and Invoices section.[3]<br/>- If you're on annual Pro billing, you cannot automatically switch to another plan mid-year through self-serve. You'll need to contact hi@cursor.com for assistance with transitions involving yearly subscriptions.[3]<br/>- Teams plans charge per seat (one seat per team member), and mid-cycle additions are charged for the remaining time in that billing period.[3]<br/><br/>**Support:**<br/>If you encounter billing issues or errors during upgrade, email hi@cursor.com from your account email address with your account details and a description of the problem.[3] |
